Description: All customer-facing pages and sections of the website
User registration, login, OAuth and profile management
Reservation form and WhatsApp integration
Customer review submission and display system
Admin panel for managing site content, menus, images
Voucher creation, flash sale scheduling, automation
Review moderation, reservation viewer, user management
Database, API, file storage, cron jobs, CI/CD setup

Summary: Hero section with cover video and brand identity
Description: As a visitor, I see a full-screen cover video when I land on the site. The video autoplays muted with the ILOT Coco Beach branding overlaid. Ken Burns effect on fallback image. GSAP staggered text entrance animation.

Summary: About section with business story and features
Description: As a visitor, I can read about the business and its unique features. Split layout: text + image with parallax. 2x2 feature cards (cabanes, eaux cristallines, hamacs, ambiance). GSAP scroll-triggered entrance.

Summary: Photo gallery with masonry grid and lightbox
Description: As a visitor, I can browse the photo gallery. Masonry CSS columns layout (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile). Clip-path reveal animation on scroll. Lightbox with prev/next navigation and keyboard support. Images fetched from API.

Summary: Menu page with Standard and Extra versions
Description: As a visitor, I can view the menu with prices and images. Two tabs or toggle: Standard / Extra. Each item shows name, description, price, image. Data fetched from API. Responsive card grid.

As a visitor, I can see available spaces: cabanes sur sable, pied dans l'eau, plage, balade pirate en mer. Each emplacement shows image, description, capacity and price. Horizontal scroll on desktop, vertical stack on mobile.
As a visitor, I can see active vouchers and promotions displayed on the site. Voucher cards show code, discount percentage, and expiry date. Fetched from /api/vouchers/active endpoint.

Summary: Flash sale section with countdown timer
Description: As a visitor, I see active flash sales with a countdown timer showing time remaining. Timer calculates client-side from ends_at timestamp. Urgency-driven design with animated countdown digits. Auto-hides when expired.

As a visitor, I can read approved customer reviews. Reviews show rating (1-5 stars), comment text, user name and date. Only reviews where is_approved=true and is_visible=true are shown. Carousel or grid layout.

As a visitor, I can click WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger buttons to contact the business. WhatsApp opens wa.me link. Instagram opens @ilotcocobeach profile. Messenger opens m.me link. Floating action buttons on mobile.

As a visitor, I can see the location on a map and get directions. Google Maps iframe embed using lat/lng from config. Contact info cards: address, phone, hours, how to get there. Directions link opens Google Maps.

As a visitor, I can switch between French, Arabic and English. Vue I18n with JSON translation files. Language switcher in header. Arabic triggers RTL layout via dir attribute. Language preference saved in localStorage.

Summary: Responsive design for all screen sizes
Description: As a visitor, the site looks great on desktop (1920px), tablet (768px) and mobile (375px). TailwindCSS responsive breakpoints. Mobile hamburger menu. Touch-friendly interactions. Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari.

Summary: User registration with email or phone
Description: As a visitor, I can sign up with email or phone number + password. POST /api/auth/register endpoint. Zod validation. bcrypt password hashing (12 rounds). Returns JWT access + refresh tokens. Duplicate check on email/phone.
As a registered user, I can log in and receive JWT tokens. Access token: 15min expiry, stored in memory. Refresh token: 7d expiry, httpOnly cookie. Auto-refresh on 401 response in API interceptor.

As a visitor, I can sign up or log in with Google or Facebook. Passport.js strategies for Google and Facebook. OAuth2 callback exchanges code for provider token. Creates or links user account. Returns JWT tokens.
As a user, I can reset my password via email. POST /api/auth/forgot-password sends reset link. Token with 1h expiry stored in DB. POST /api/auth/reset-password validates token and updates password.

As a visitor, I can fill a reservation form and send it via WhatsApp. Fields: name, phone, date, guests, message, voucher code. Form validates required fields (shake animation on error). Builds pre-formatted WhatsApp message and opens wa.me URL. Also saves reservation to database via POST /api/reservations.

As a user, I can enter a voucher code in the reservation form. Frontend sends code to POST /api/vouchers/validate. API checks: exists, is_active, not expired, uses < max_uses. Returns discount info or error. Discount shown in the form before sending.

As a logged-in user, I can view my past reservations. GET /api/reservations/mine (authenticated). Shows date, guests, status (pending/confirmed/cancelled), voucher used. Simple list view in user profile.

Summary: Submit a review as logged-in user
Description: As a logged-in user, I can submit a review with rating and text. POST /api/reviews (authenticated). Rating: 1-5 stars (clickable star component). Comment: text area. Review saved with is_approved=false by default. Success message: 'Merci! Votre avis sera visible apres validation.'

As a visitor, I only see reviews approved by admin. GET /api/reviews returns WHERE is_approved=true AND is_visible=true. Frontend displays rating stars, comment, user name. No edit/delete from frontend.

As an admin, I can log in to the backoffice securely. Same JWT auth but role check. Admin routes protected by middleware: req.user.role === 'admin'. Admin dashboard at /admin route. Redirect to login if not authenticated.

As an admin, I can add, edit and delete menu items and categories. CRUD endpoints: POST/PUT/DELETE /api/admin/menu/categories and /items. Form: name (FR/EN/AR), description, price_standard, price_extra, image upload, is_available toggle. Drag-to-reorder with sort_order update.

Summary: Admin image management for all sections
Description: As an admin, I can upload, reorder and delete images for all site sections. Upload to Cloudflare R2 via /api/admin/images. Section selector: hero, gallery, menu, emplacement. Grid view with drag-to-reorder. Delete with confirmation dialog.

Summary: Admin hero video and cover content editor
Description: As an admin, I can change the hero video and cover text. Upload video to R2. Edit overlay text via site_settings table. Preview before publishing.
As an admin, I can edit emplacement info: descriptions, images, pricing and capacity. CRUD for emplacements table. Trilingual text fields. Image upload. Sort order management.

Summary: Admin site settings and SEO editor
Description: As an admin, I can edit contact info, about text and SEO keywords. Key-value pairs in site_settings table. Editable fields: phone, email, address, lat/lng, about_text_fr/en/ar, meta_title, meta_description, meta_keywords. Changes reflect immediately on public site.

As an admin, I can create vouchers with code, discount and expiry. CRUD at /api/admin/vouchers. Fields: code (auto-generate or manual), discount_percent or discount_amount, valid_from, valid_until, max_uses. Table view with active/inactive status. Toggle activate/deactivate.
As an admin, I can manually activate or deactivate any voucher. PUT /api/admin/vouchers/:id/toggle. Toggle button in voucher list. Overrides cron job status if needed.

As an admin, I can create flash sales with start time, end time and discount. CRUD at /api/admin/flash-sales. Fields: title (FR/EN/AR), description, discount_percent, starts_at (datetime picker), ends_at (datetime picker), image. Preview of how countdown will look.

The system automatically activates flash sales at their start time and deactivates at end time. node-cron job runs every minute. Checks starts_at <= NOW() for activation. Checks ends_at <= NOW() for deactivation. Updates is_active flag in database.

Summary: Admin view and moderate all reviews
Description: As an admin, I can see all submitted reviews regardless of status. GET /api/admin/reviews returns all reviews. Table view with: user name, rating, comment, date, status (pending/approved/rejected).

As an admin, I can approve, reject or hide any review. PUT /api/admin/reviews/:id with action: approve (is_approved=true, is_visible=true), reject (is_approved=false), hide (is_visible=false). Bulk actions for multiple reviews.

As an admin, I can see all reservation requests. GET /api/admin/reservations. Table: name, phone, date, guests, voucher used, status, created_at. Filter by date range and status. Click to view details.
As an admin, I can see all registered users. GET /api/admin/users. Table: name, email/phone, provider, registration date. Search and filter. No edit/delete for privacy (admin can only view).

Summary: Database schema design and Prisma migrations
Description: Set up PostgreSQL database with all 10 tables. Prisma schema with models: User, MenuCategory, MenuItem, Emplacement, Image, Voucher, FlashSale, Review, Reservation, SiteSetting. Relations and indexes. Initial seed data for settings and admin user.

Summary: REST API scaffold with auth middleware
Description: Set up Fastify server with route structure. JWT auth middleware (access + refresh tokens). Role-based access control (public, user, admin). CORS configuration. Error handling. Request validation with Zod. Rate limiting.

Summary: File storage setup with Cloudflare R2
Description: Set up S3-compatible file upload to Cloudflare R2. Multer middleware for multipart uploads. Image validation (max size, allowed types). Auto-generate unique filenames. Return public URL after upload. Delete file on image removal.

Summary: Cron jobs for flash sales and voucher expiry
Description: Set up node-cron scheduled tasks. Flash sale cron: every minute, activate/deactivate based on timestamps. Voucher cron: every hour, deactivate expired or maxed-out vouchers. Logging for job execution. Error handling with retry.

Set up automated deployment pipeline. GitHub Actions workflow: lint, test, build on push to main. Auto-deploy frontend to Vercel. Auto-deploy backend to Railway (or Docker build for VPS). Environment variable management.
